name: Structured Concurrency
def: Scopes the lifetime of concurrent tasks to a lexical block: children cannot outlive their parent scope.
problem: Tame "fire-and-forget" task sprawl, leaks, and lost errors in async/parallel code.
mechanism: A nursery/scope spawns tasks and awaits all of them on exit; errors and cancellation propagate to the whole group.
limits: Slightly less flexible than free spawning
failures: Detached tasks escaping the scope (defeats the model)
Cancellation not honoured by leaf work
myths: "Just a coding style" — it is enforced lifetime scoping with cancellation semantics
